name: Entrebarrancos Trail
name_original: Barranco de La Noguera — circular desde Alcalá del Júcar
description: Hike the moderate 5.6-mile Entrebarrancos Trail near Alcalá del Júcar, offering stunning views of the village and its castle.
region_intro: Hiking around Alcalá Del Júcar offers exploration of a distinctive landscape shaped by the Júcar River. The region is characterized by a deep gorge carved by the river, winding through impressive rock walls and mountainous terrain. This area features towering cliffs and a unique village integrated into the rocky slopes, providing varied hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Alcalá Del Júcar The most popular hiking route is Entrebarrancos Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route provides views of the village and its castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is The cliffs of Tolosa — circular, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This circular route offers scenic views of the Tolosa cliffs. Local hikers also love the Las Eras, Cañon del Júcar, El Morrón, Tolosa — circular desde Alcala del Júcar, a 14.6 miles (23.4 km) trail leading through the Júcar Canyon and mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Alcalá Del Júcar is defined by the Júcar River gorge, dramatic cliffs, and mountainous terrain. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Alcalá Del Júcar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcalá Del Júcar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcalá Del Júcar?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Alcalá Del Júcar, offering a variety of experiences. These include 6 easy routes, 5 moderate, and 4 difficult options, ensuring there's a path for every fitness level.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Alcalá Del Júcar?
A: Hiking in Alcalá Del Júcar offers dramatic and varied landscapes. You'll primarily encounter the stunning gorge carved by the Júcar River, with its impressive rock walls and winding meanders. The area is also characterized by towering cliffs and mountainous terrain, with the unique village itself clinging to and carved into the rocky slopes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Alcalá Del Júcar offers several easy hiking trails perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. One such option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087851338/the-cliffs-of-tolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The cliffs of Tolosa — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3-mile (5.3 km) path that provides scenic views without significant challenge.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Alcalá Del Júcar?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Alcalá Del Júcar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087851338/the-cliffs-of-tolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The cliffs of Tolosa — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1074843258/las-eras-canon-del-jucar-el-morron-tolosa-circular-desde-alcala-del-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Eras, Cañon del Júcar, El Morrón, Tolosa — circular desde Alcala del Júcar&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer, more strenuous loop through the canyon.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks to see along the hiking trails?
A: The trails often lead to or pass by several unique attractions. You can explore the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Caves&lt;/a&gt;, which are carved into the rock, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alcalá del Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, perched high above the village. Many routes also offer breathtaking views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge&lt;/a&gt; from various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Alcalá Del Júcar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic scenery of the Júcar River gorge, the unique architecture of the village, and the diverse range of trails that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult hikes in the region?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1074843258/las-eras-canon-del-jucar-el-morron-tolosa-circular-desde-alcala-del-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Eras, Cañon del Júcar, El Morrón, Tolosa — circular desde Alcala del Júcar&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 14.6-mile (23.4 km) trail that takes you through the Júcar Canyon and mountainous terrain, requiring good fitness.

Q: Is it possible to reach Alcalá Del Júcar by public transport for hiking?
A: While Alcalá Del Júcar is a somewhat remote village, there are bus services connecting it to larger towns like Albacete. However, public transport options directly to specific trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance if you plan to rely on public transport for your hiking trip.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Alcalá Del Júcar?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Alcalá Del Júcar. Due to the village's unique layout on a rocky slope, parking can be found in designated areas, often at the entrance or slightly outside the most central, narrow streets. It's recommended to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the unique cave houses?
A: Many trails and viewpoints around Alcalá Del Júcar offer excellent opportunities to see the distinctive cave houses and the village's architecture. As you ascend towards the castle or explore paths overlooking the river, you'll get panoramic views of these homes carved directly into the rock face, blending seamlessly with the natural environment.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcalá Del Júcar?
A: The best time for hiking in Alcalá Del Júcar is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though hiking is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.
tips: Alcalá del Júcar is located in the northwest of the province of Albacete, within the region of La Manchuela. This municipality was declared a Historical-Artistic Site in 1982. In 1986 it was awarded the third prize, after the Eiffel Tower and the Great Mosque of Istanbul, for the best artistic lighting.

faqs.question: Where does the Entrebarrancos Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Entrebarrancos Trail starts and ends in the charming town of Alcalá del Júcar. As it's a popular starting point for various routes, you'll typically find parking options within or on the outskirts of the town.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike is approximately 9 kilometers long and generally takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, the Entrebarrancos Trail involves some elevation gain and varied terrain. While not overly strenuous, beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should be comfortable, but be prepared for some uneven surfaces typical of ravine trails.

faqs.question: Is the route a loop or an out-and-back trail?
faqs.answer: The Entrebarrancos Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, allowing you to experience diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characteristic of 'barrancos' (ravines). You can expect a mix of footpaths and tracks, potentially with uneven surfaces. The route winds through natural landscapes, offering transitions between different environments.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several scenic highlights. You'll enjoy stunning views of Alcalá del Júcar and its impressive castle, including a specific viewpoint of Alcalá del Júcar Castle. You'll also pass by the Río Júcar Riverside Beach in Alcalá del Júcar.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows the Entrebarrancos trail for a significant portion (67%). It also briefly intersects with sections of the Ruta del Agua and the historic camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: Is the Entrebarrancos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail aren't detailed, many trails in natural areas of Spain are dog-friendly, especially if kept on a leash. It's always best to ensure your dog is well-behaved and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summers can be very hot, so if hiking then, start early to avoid midday heat. Winters are usually mild but can be damp.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to hike the Entrebarrancos Trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or services near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail starts in Alcalá del Júcar, a well-known town, you'll find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options before or after your hike.
